rationale: Educ. Code §45.108 governs school district short-term borrowing capped at 75% of prior-year income. Increasing the homestead exemption indirectly reduces district tax revenue but does not alter any operative text or requirement of this section.
quote: except that the loans may not at any time exceed 75 percent of the previous year's income
_note: No direct textual conflict. Increased homestead exemption would indirectly reduce district property tax revenue, potentially lowering borrowing capacity under the 75% income cap, but §45.108 contains no provision tied to the homestead exemption amount in §11.13(b).
